items.title: Intel's Stock Soars 24% Friday, Its Biggest One-Day Gain Since 1987
items.description: Intel's stock price soared 24% Friday. It's the stock's largest single-day spike since since October 1987, reports CNBC, "as investors cheered signs of renewed growth due to mounting artificial intelligence demand." The stock closed at $82.57 and is

items.title: Physicists Revive 1990s Laser Concept To Propose a Next-Generation Atomic Clock

items.title: Maine Governor Vetoes Data Center Moratorium Bill
items.description: Maine Gov. Janet Mills vetoed a bill that would have imposed the nation's first statewide moratorium on new data centers, saying she supported the idea in principle but would not block a major redevelopment project tied to jobs and local investment.

items.title: BMW Is One Step Closer To Selling You a Color-Changing Car

items.title: OpenAI CEO apologizes to Tumbler Ridge community
items.description: In a letter to the residents of Tumbler Ridge, Canada, OpenAI CEO Sam Altman said he is “deeply sorry” that his company failed to alert law enforcement about the suspect in a recent mass shooting.

items.title: The climate tech IPO window could finally be cracking open
items.description: Nuclear startup X-energy went public, geothermal startup Fervo is about to. Could this be the moment that climate tech investors have been waiting for?

items.title: Why Tokyo is the most important tech destination of 2026
items.description: SusHi Tech Tokyo 2026 has four tightly defined technology domains, each backed by live demonstrations, dedicated exhibit floors, and sessions featuring the people actually building and funding these technologies globally.

items.title: Apps to distract you from the endless cycle of doomscrolling
items.description: It's hard to break the cycle of doomscrolling, but there are plenty of apps that can help you spend more time on content that’s engaging and productive.

items.title: Apple under Ternus: what comes next for the tech giant’s hardware strategy
items.description: John Ternus, Apple's incoming CEO, is a hardware guy, signaling Apple may be putting devices back at the center of its strategy.

items.title: India’s Snabbit seeks fresh funding at a $400M valuation, sources say
items.description: Snabbit has scaled rapidly, crossing one million jobs in March, amid growing investor interest.

items.description: This round, should it occur, would double the house-help startup's valuation in a matter of weeks.

items.description: Steve Ballmer wrote a fiery letter in the sentencing of disgraced founder Joseph Sanberg documenting all the harm that's befalling him as an investor.

items.title: Palantir is reportedly helping the IRS investigate financial crimes
items.description: The IRS has used Palantir's software since at least 2018, The Intercept reports.

items.title: Cohere acquires, merges with Germany-based startup to create a ‘transatlantic AI powerhouse’
items.description: Cohere, the Canada-based AI company that makes AI tools for businesses in regulated industries, announced Friday it would merge with Aleph Alpha, a German company that also builds AI systems for businesses and governments.

items.description: Series, a social networking app that's grown popular on college campuses, announced a $5.1 million pre-seed round from some big names in tech.

items.title: Meta’s loss is Thinking Machines’ gain
items.description: Meta has been poaching talent from Thinking Machines Lab. But it's a two-way street.

items.description: Investors flocked to nuclear power startup X-energy in its first day of public trading on the Nasdaq.

items.title: ComfyUI hits $500M valuation as creators seek more control over AI-generated media
items.description: ComfyUI, whose tools give creators more control over AI image, video, and audio generation, just raised $30 million.

items.title: X launches stand-alone XChat app on iOS
items.description: X's new app promises private chats, disappearing messages, audio and video calls, and more.

items.title: Google to invest up to $40B in Anthropic in cash and compute
items.description: Google plans up to $40B investment in Anthropic as AI rivals race to secure massive compute capacity, following the limited release of its powerful, cybersecurity-focused Mythos model.

items.title: Marked-up Mac minis flood eBay amid shortages driven by AI
items.description: Apple’s sold-out Mac mini is spawning marked-up eBay listings as demand surges for the compact desktop, now favored for running local AI models and tools.

items.title: Nuclear startup X-energy raises $1B in data center-driven IPO
items.description: Amazon-backed X-energy was able to raise around 20% more money in its IPO as demand for nuclear power surges.

items.title: Another spyware maker caught distributing fake Android snooping apps
items.description: Researchers have found a new case where government authorities used a fake Android app to plant spyware on a target’s phone. The company that allegedly developed the spyware was not previously known to sell this type of software.
